label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to solve this problem step by step. So, we have five people on a soccer team: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. At the start of the match, each has a specific position. Then, there are several swaps between pairs of players. Our goal is to figure out where Bob ends up at the end of all these swaps. First, I need to list out the original positions: - Alice: right winger - Bob: right midfielder - Claire: left winger - Dave: cheerleader - Eve: goalkeeper Then, there are a series of swaps. Let me go through each one carefully. First swap: Dave and Bob trade positions. So, let's track each person's position after each swap. Original: - Alice: right winger - Bob: right midfielder - Claire: left winger - Dave: cheerleader - Eve: goalkeeper After first swap (Dave and Bob): - Alice: right winger - Bob: cheerleader (because he swapped with Dave) - Claire: left winger - Dave: right midfielder (because he swapped with Bob) - Eve: goalkeeper Wait, no, that's not right. If Dave and Bob swap, then Dave (originally cheerleader) swaps with Bob (originally right midfielder). So after the swap, Bob is at cheerleader and Dave is at right midfielder. Correct. Second swap: Dave and Eve trade positions. So now Dave is at right midfielder and Eve is at goalkeeper. After swapping, Dave takes Eve's position (goalkeeper) and Eve takes Dave's position (right midfielder). So after second swap: - Alice: right winger - Bob: cheerleader - Claire: left winger - Dave: goalkeeper - Eve: right midfielder Third swap: Alice and Bob trade positions. Currently, Alice is right winger and Bob is cheerleader. After swapping, Alice becomes cheerleader and Bob becomes right winger. After third swap: - Alice: cheerleader - Bob: right winger - Claire: left winger - Dave: goalkeeper - Eve: right midfielder Fourth swap: Dave and Claire
